source_pr.title: spool: fix unstable test TestPoolTuneScaleUpAndDown
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #42163

Problem Summary:

Some tasks are still increasing after ```Tune```, so we need to wait for all tasks to be completed.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: The test used `time.Sleep(...)` to wait for workers/tasks after `p.Tune(...)`, which is timing-sensitive and can intermittently observe the wrong `p.Running()` value.
failure_signature: expect 8 workers running, but got <n> (timing-dependent after `Tune` without deterministic wait)
root_cause_explanation: `TestPoolTuneScaleUpAndDown` was flaky because it relied on a fixed sleep to wait for goroutines to start/finish after calling `Tune`, and the sleep duration was not deterministic under CI load. This PR stabilizes the test by replacing `time.Sleep(500ms)` with `wg.Wait()` so the test waits for the goroutines/tasks to complete before asserting on `p.Running()`.
fix_pattern: Replace fixed sleeps used for synchronization with deterministic coordination (`WaitGroup.Wait`, channels, or Eventually with a condition) before asserting on concurrent state.

evidence: evidence: spool: fix unstable test TestPoolTuneScaleUpAndDown
